column1: salmon + rice/soy sauce — simple Japanese direction

column6: {"recipes":[{"name":"Gochujang Sesame Tofu Stir-Fry","emoji":"🍜","desc":"Sweet-spicy tofu with sesame aroma","add":[{"name_en":"soy sauce"},{"name_en":"garlic"},{"name_en":"green onions"}]},{"name":"Crispy Baked Gochujang Tofu Bites","emoji":"🔥","desc":"Sticky glaze, crunchy outside, tender inside","add":[{"name_en":"cornstarch"},{"name_en":"brown sugar"},{"name_en":"rice vinegar"}]},{"name":"Tofu Rice Bowl with Sesame Gochujang Sauce","emoji":"🥗","desc":"Warm rice bowl with tangy tofu","add":[{"name_en":"cucumber"},{"name_en":"carrot"},{"name_en":"soy sauce"}]}]}
column10: All three recipes are practical home-cooking and clearly center tofu. The added ingredients avoid gochujang, sesame oil, and rice. Descriptions are concise and under 8 words. The recipes are Korean-influenced due to gochujang and sesame flavoring, but only one or possibly two are recognizably Korean dishes; names like 'stir-fry' and 'rice bowl' are generic rather than clearly Korean dish types, so the requirement that at least 2 of 3 be recognizably Korean is not fully satisfied.
column16: While the recipes are practical, use tofu as a key ingredient, don't duplicate user's existing ingredients, and have concise descriptions, they fail the Korean-influence requirement. Only Recipe 1 (Gochujang Stir-fry) is recognizably Korean. Recipe 2 (with avocado, cucumber, sriracha) is a generic fusion bowl, and Recipe 3 (with pineapple and lime) is tropical/fusion. The rubric requires at least 2 of 3 to be recognizably Korean dishes, which is not met.
column22: While the recipes are practical, use tofu as a key ingredient, avoid listing already-owned ingredients, and have concise descriptions, they fail the Korean-influence requirement. Only 1 of 3 recipes (Baked Gochujang Tofu) is recognizably Korean. The other two are generic (Crispy Tofu Steak, Silken Tofu Soup) despite the user having gochujang and sesame oil, which should drive Korean-influenced suggestions.

column1: avocado + no other items — should produce diverse recipes
column16: Recipes are practical and avocado-centric, descriptions are concise (≤8 words). However, diversity is weak: the shrimp salad (lime, cilantro) and grilled chicken with avocado salsa (jalapeño, cilantro) are both Latin/Mexican-influenced. Only the avocado toast represents a distinctly different style. The rubric emphasizes diversity as 'the key test,' and two of three recipes share a very similar cuisine profile.

column1: chicken thigh + Mexican items + vegetarian profile — conflict handling
column16: The model provided vegetarian alternatives but did NOT explicitly acknowledge the conflict between the user's chicken thigh request and their vegetarian dietary restriction. The rubric states 'ignoring the conflict entirely is NOT acceptable' — while the model implicitly applied the vegetarian restriction, it never noted or addressed the tension, which constitutes ignoring the conflict. Other criteria (no duplicate ingredients, mostly Mexican-leaning, concise descriptions) are met.

column1: bok choy + garlic/oyster sauce — authentic Chinese, not fusion
column6: {"recipes":[{"name":"Garlic Oyster Bok Choy Stir-Fry","emoji":"🥢","desc":"Savory quick stir-fry, tender-crisp bok choy","add":[{"name_en":"sesame oil"},{"name_en":"white pepper"},{"name_en":"cornstarch"}]},{"name":"Oyster Sauce Braised Bok Choy (Quick Simmer)","emoji":"🍲","desc":"Silky braise with garlicky oyster goodness","add":[{"name_en":"chicken stock"},{"name_en":"light soy sauce"},{"name_en":"sugar"}]},{"name":"Bok Choy Garlic-Oyster Sheet Pan Roast","emoji":"🔥","desc":"Roasted edges, glossy garlic-oyster finish","add":[{"name_en":"chili flakes"},{"name_en":"olive oil"},{"name_en":"toasted sesame seeds"}]}]}
column22: Only 1 of 3 recipes (the stir-fry) is recognizably Chinese. 'Creamy Bok Choy Soup' with heavy cream and 'Roasted Bok Choy with Garlic' using olive oil and lemon are clearly Western/fusion dishes. The rubric requires at least 2 of 3 to be recognizably Chinese and explicitly states recipes should not be westernized. This is a significant failure on the core authenticity requirements.
